item.title: World Cup final: Lamine Yamal, Spain's golden boy on the verge of teenage immortality
item.description: As Spain takes the field in the World Cup final, it will be led by a teenage sensation like few we have ever seen. This is the Lamine Yamal story so far.

item.title: Moment Andrew Tate was arrested and handcuffed in Miami
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Bek left a doomsday church at 27. Here's what her life looks like now
item.description: Author Bek Condello says she has not spoken to her friends or family inside the church since she decided to leave.

item.title: Tom Trbojevic gutted after another injury, Titans, Dolphins record wins
item.description: The Dolphins shook off last week's humiliating thrashing to power past the Cowboys 36-16 at Lang Park, after Tom Trbojevic suffered another hamstring injury in Manly's defeat to Gold Coast.

item.title: Actor Terence Donovan dies aged 90 after more than half a century on Australian screens
item.description: Jason Donovan has paid tribute to his father who has died at the age of 90 after decades on Australian screens.

item.title: Secret Harbour by-election: Meet the candidates vying for your vote
item.description: The Secret Harbour by-election is shaping up as one of the most closely-watched political contests in WA for some time. Here's everything you need to know about the candidates and how to vote.

item.title: World Cup: Argentina fans stage staggering Times Square takeover ahead of final against Spain
item.description: Times Square has been the epicentre for World Cup celebrations throughout the tournament, but it reached new heights as Argentina fans swamped the "centre of the universe" ahead of the final against Spain in nearby New Jersey.

item.title: US military releases vision purported to be strikes on Iranian military targets
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Store-bought vodka from licensed IGA retailer found to contain key ingredient in paint stripper
item.description: Australia's bootleg booze market is booming and an alarming new discovery from a bottle of vodka sold in Melbourne reveals it is turning into a major health problem.

item.title: Hundreds of activists join Arrernte traditional owners ahead of Pine Gap protest
item.description: Hundreds of activists and academics have gathered in Alice Springs to join Arrernte traditional owners ahead of a planned protest against the joint Australian-US military base at Pine Gap.

item.title: Special guest dazzles aspiring young astronauts at Adelaide's Space Day Out
item.description: Young South Australians got one step closer to achieving their space exploration dreams on Sunday when they met a "real-life" astronaut.

item.title: Russia unleashes one of biggest ballistic missile attacks of the Ukraine war, Kyiv says
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Russia battered Ukraine's capital Kyiv overnight into Sunday local time, with one of its biggest ballistic missile barrages of the war.

item.title: Russia bombards Kyic with ballistic missiles
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Federal government to tighten rules around use of AI in departmental decision-making
item.description: Agencies like Centrelink and Services Australia will have to follow a framework to ensure decision-making is "fair and accurate" if they use AI.

item.title: Pauline Hanson says she is 'not backing away' from Tommy Robinson interview
item.description: The One Nation leader was a guest on the podcast of controversial far-right UK figure Tommy Robinson, where she lamented the end of the White Australia policy.

item.title: World Cup final ends in glory for Spain and heartache for champion Messi
item.description: Spain wins the 2026 World Cup, with Ferran Torres coming off the bench to score the winner in extra time against 10-man Argentina.

item.title: Race Around the World recap: Catch the week seven films
item.description: From Mexican wrestling to Indian slums, catch up on every week seven film from Race Around the World.

item.title: This week in history quiz, July 20: Test your smarts on the first Moon landing, John Howard and the Riot Act
item.description: Consider yourself a history buff? Test your smarts on the first Moon landing, John Howard and the Riot Act.

item.title: How you can get the balance right when being online is your job
item.description: When being across trends in your industry feels like part of your job it can feel like work never ends. Here's how to protect your free time.

item.title: Telehealth peptide prescriptions expose longevity industry stretching the rules
item.description: The booming industry selling Australians on treatments to live longer is exploiting significant gaps in health regulation, exposing consumers to risk.

item.title: Tony Abbott isn't helping the Liberals with their Pauline Hanson problem
item.description: Pauline Hanson's grievance politics is a challenge for Labor, but a splitting headache for the Liberals — compounded by former prime minister Tony Abbott.

item.title: Humanoid robot markets expand as Australia reckons with AI revolution
item.description: Australia this week made an important step forward in grappling with the AI revolution. Meanwhile, in Melbourne a company is making humanoid robots.

item.title: ABC Your Say invites people across the country to tell us what you think
item.description: Sulaiman Zandinan says Australia is amazing. Wendy Newman says people in the bush are "not country bumpkins". The ABC is inviting you to have your say about the issues, challenges and opportunities that shape your life in 2026.

item.title: Doctors worry health system can't make up for loss of services if Hobart Private sells to Catholic-run Calvary
item.description: Doctors are concerned Tasmanians will be forced to travel interstate for certain procedures if the Hobart Private Hospital is sold to Catholic provider Calvary, but the health minister insists there will be no service gap.

item.title: Paramedics told to take complex cardiac patients to Launceston General Hospital instead of Mersey
item.description: Paramedics have been told to transport complex cardiac patients in Tasmania's north-west to the Launceston General Hospital rather than the local Mersey Community Hospital.

item.title: Council of Europe questions World Cup's integrity after controversial 2026 tournament
item.description: Europe's top human rights body hits out at FIFA over the integrity of the World Cup and calls for change before the 2030 tournament.

item.title: Lesbian couples considered separate families in updated Queensland Health fertility clinic guidance
item.description: Queensland Health advice, given to fertility providers to help navigate the state's assisted reproductive technology legislation, has had a "devastating" impact on some same-sex couples trying to have more children.

item.title: Queensland set for classic winter week, hazardous surf in south-east to weaken
item.description: The Bureau of Meteorology says showers from the Wide Bay to the Gold Coast are possible today, with hazardous surf conditions forecast to weaken.

item.title: World Cup winners Spain will receive championship rings, but almost 2,000 of them will be bought by the public
item.description: World Cup winners Spain will receive championship rings, but so will almost 2,000 people who have enough money to buy them.

item.title: Play goes beyond the archives to revive an Adelaide street's lost history
item.description: It's been a three year labour of love probing into the past and building on the fragments of a hidden history to write Logan St and get it to the stage.

item.title: The wait for unbuilt social housing felt as Sydney's homeless 'still out on the street'
item.description: People with lived experience of homelessness say the issue is deepening as housing stock remains out of reach and community providers are left struggling to cope with demand.

item.title: Hundreds of Queensland school support staff lodge complaints about violence
item.description: About four hundred staff — the majority teacher aides — have complained to the United Workers Union since April about facing violent behaviour from students and parents.

item.title: Migrant DFV victim-survivors made to prove genuineness of relationship
item.description: A federal government safety net has been identified as one of the biggest stressors for migrant women experiencing domestic and family violence.

item.title: ASX slips, oil jumps, markets remain on edge as war and AI worries drag down sentiment — as it happened
item.description: The ASX edged lower as markets fret about war and inflated AI valuations. Look back on how the session unfolded on our markets blog.

item.title: Woman dies after unit fire in Buderim on Sunshine Coast
item.description: A 21-year-old woman has died in hospital after suffering extensive burns during an overnight unit fire on the Sunshine Coast.

item.title: Jonas Vingegaard out of Tour de France after fracturing collarbone in 'devastating' crash
item.description: Jonas Vingegaard's Tour de France campaign comes to an end after a crash on the 15th stage.

item.title: Taylor responds to Liberal's praise for Malcolm Roberts — as it happened
item.description: Angus Taylor distances himself from a Liberal senator's endorsement of One Nation's Malcolm Roberts.

item.title: US boxer Hannah Rapp dies after being hit by car while cycling
item.description: An up-and-coming American boxer, Hannah Rapp, has died after being hit by a car while cycling.

item.title: Endangered bettongs reintroduced in WA
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Former Port Adelaide coach Ken Hinkley announced as Tasmania Devils inaugural coach
item.description: The former Port Adelaide coach is formally announced as the inaugural coach of the Tasmania Devils. It completes a year-long hunt for a senior coach for the AFL's newest club.

item.title: 'Rat kangaroos' reintroduced to Dirk Hartog Island as researchers get hands dirty
item.description: Researchers were unafraid to get their hands "dirty" in an ambitious bid to restore WA's largest island to its pre-colonial state.

item.title: What is the offside rule? Why Spain's goal was disallowed in the World Cup final
item.description: Wondering why that Ferran Torres goal in extra time of the World Cup final was disallowed? We're here to help.

item.title: Football Victoria condemns Preston Lions fans over conduct at South Melbourne game
item.description: A professional football club in Melbourne's north faces calls to ban hardline supporters who brandished sexist and "ethnically derogatory" banners at a match on Saturday.

item.title: US launches more strikes on Iran and IRGC attacks oil tankers in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US launches a new round of air strikes on Iran after announcing the death of another American service member, and the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says it has immobilised two oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: World Cup final quick hits: Argentina lose heads in defeat, Trump crashes Spain's party
item.description: Argentina's players lash out in defeat, Ferran Torres becomes a Spanish icon forever and Donald Trump attempts to crash the winners' party. Here are the World Cup final quick hits.

item.title: Victoria Premier Jacinta Allan says 'witch' billboard drives disrespect for women
item.description: The Victorian premier says a controversial billboard seen around Melbourne again is "dangerous" and driving disrespect for women.

item.title: Jennifer Finch, bassist for grunge rock feminists L7, dies at 59
item.description: Grunge trailblazers L7 honour their "beloved bandmate, friend and fellow troublemaker", who died just five days after revealing she had been diagnosed with aggressive brain cancer.

item.title: Is AI changing your life in unexpected ways? Should we move faster or more cautiously? The ABC wants to hear from you
item.description: Most of us never chose to live in an artificial intelligence (AI) world. Yet over the past few years, it has become part of everyday life, changing how we think, learn and communicate.

item.title: Australia-India export deal triggers debate over uranium opportunities in Broken Hill
item.description: Australia's trade deal to supply India with unenriched uranium sparks conversations about potential new mining opportunities in Broken Hill.

item.title: AI wildlife warning system trial aims to reduce animal-vehicle collisions on Monaro Highway near Canberra
item.description: An estimated 10 million animals are killed on Australian roads each year. It's hoped a new artificial intelligence-powered alert system being trialled on the Monaro Highway near Canberra will reduce the toll.

item.title: Essendon star Nate Caddy cleared to face Hawthorn despite abrupt hospital visit
item.description: Nate Caddy is expected to fully train and play next weekend despite experiencing an elevated heart rate during Essendon's win over GWS.

item.title: Derryn Hinch's brother remembers 'colourful, constantly changing' life — as it happened
item.description: Derryn Hinch, the outspoken and at times controversial broadcaster and former senator, is being farewelled at a state funeral service in Melbourne after his death earlier this month, aged 82.

item.title: 13 photos that sum up the chaos of the 2026 World Cup final between Spain and Argentina
item.description: It was magical chaos from start to finish as Spain claimed victory in the 2026 World Cup final. Here are the 13 photos that summed it up.

item.title: Key takeaways from random FIFA World Cup half-time show
item.description: Superstar performers at the inaugural FIFA World Cup half-time show included Madonna, Justin Bieber, Shakira and BTS, with Australian Emmanuel Kelly part of the fold.

item.title: Woman in serious condition as police investigate stabbing in Kalgoorlie
item.description: Part of the mining city's main street was blocked off and detectives are investigating after the woman was found with serious stab wounds.

item.title: Tears streaming, Lionel Messi departs as a legend despite World Cup final defeat
item.description: As the champion wept on the pitch after the World Cup final defeat, Argentina may well have wondered with trepidation what life will look like in the post-Messi era.

item.title: Baby Thea Flaskett's death at Redcliffe Hospital caused by congenital heart defect, inquest finds
item.description: Thea Flaskett died four hours after she was born via vacuum assisted delivery at Redcliffe Hospital in September 2023.

item.title: US military releases footage it claims shows strikes against Iranian targets
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Research video shows gorilla laughing after being tickled
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Maclean's fishing co-op site to be sold amid white spot disease fallout
item.description: A seafood outlet that has been operating for almost 80 years on the NSW Mid North Coast will go up for auction as the Clarence River Fishermen's Co-op battles financial challenges posed by white spot disease regulations.

item.title: Individual stars lit up World Cup but Spain's triumph a reminder collective spirit trumps all
item.description: As some of football's biggest names lit up the World Cup, Spain flew under the radar all the way to hoisting the trophy on its final day.

item.title: Albanese dashes hopes for new NT hospital during $10m maternity announcement
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese announces $10 million to improve maternity services at Royal Darwin Hospital, but maternity care advocates say the money is unlikely to improve staffing issues.

item.title: Ken Hinkley discusses his excitement at being named Devils coach.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Derryn Hinch remembered for sense of justice, outspoken and likeable personality at state funeral
item.description: Broadcaster Ray Martin, Australian actress Jacki Weaver and performer Rhonda Burchmore are among the entertainment heavyweights to pay tribute to Derryn Hinch at his state funeral service.

item.title: Trial begins for UK YouTuber Yung Filly accused of violent sexual assault of young fan in Perth
item.description: A Perth court hears disturbing details of the YouTube personality's alleged sexual assault of a young woman who had a "celebrity crush" on him.

item.title: Four easy one-pot dinners that are perfect for winter
item.description: From butter beans with jammy leaks to a quick chicken soup, these recipes are for you if you're on hunt for a low-effort, tasty weeknight meal.

item.title: Australia's Bernard Tomic wins first Challenger Tour title in eight years
item.description: Veteran tennis player Bernard Tomic continues to toil on the world circuit and is rewarded with his first Challenger Tour title in eight years.

item.title: Imalu Tigers, Tapalinga Super Stars crowned premiers at 2026 Tiwi Islands football grand finals
item.description: The Imalu Tigers and Tapalinga Super Stars are the new premiers of Tiwi Islands football, claiming victory in the men's and women's competitions in electric grand final matches on Sunday.

item.title: Author Randa Abdel-Fattah sues Bendigo council, La Trobe University over Bendigo Writers Festival
item.description: Randa Abdel-Fattah launches legal action after Bendigo Writers Festival organisers sent a code of conduct that sparked censorship concerns and led to the boycott of dozens of authors.

item.title: Telehealth ban still standing between dying patients and their doctors
item.description: A federal law that threatens doctors if they use electronic communications, such as the internet or phone, to discuss voluntary assisted dying remains in force, even though telehealth is now an established part of healthcare.

item.title: SA's largest electorate to be renamed in honour of Aboriginal leader
item.description: South Australia's largest federal electorate will be renamed in honour of an Aboriginal leader. Experts say it is also the state's most likely to fall to One Nation.

item.title: Tribunal upholds three-match ban for Crows forward Darcy Fogarty
item.description: The Crows fail in their challenge to Darcy Fogarty's three-match striking ban at the AFL Tribunal, as the panel finds Fogarty had time to decide to swing his arm at Sydney's Harry Cunningham. Follow live.

item.title: LGBTQIA+ nightclub's lease at former Sydney church terminated due to 'offensive trade'
item.description: Organisers of Divine Playhouse were served a breach notice earlier this month, in the wake of complaints from some members of the Christian community.

item.title: Brett McMaster avoids jail for trafficking cocaine while Australian Army soldier
item.description: The former rifleman and field medic sourced cocaine from a fellow Townsville soldier and on-sold the drugs to army colleagues.

item.title: Supporters of India's youth-led 'cockroach' movement caned by police in attempt to march on parliament
item.description: Thousands of supporters of India's youth-led "cockroach" movement gathered in New Delhi to march ‌on parliament seeking the resignation of the education minister, despite police denying permission for the protest.

item.title: Waste burning trial at Adbri cement site prompts backlash from council
item.description: A major cement manufacturer has been allowed to burn more plastic waste for fuel at its Adelaide site in a decision a local council is seeking to challenge in court.

item.title: Monday Finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Indian police pushing 'Cockroach' movement protesters
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dirt cheap and toxic alcohol being sold
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Andy Burnham becomes British PM, provides AUKUS hints in first speech
item.description: Andy Burnham has pledged to provide immediate cost-of-living relief for Brits and invited US President Donald Trump to Manchester in the hours after becoming UK PM on Monday.

item.title: Having a spouse with dementia may increase your own risk of condition, study finds
item.description: Having a spouse with dementia may put you at a higher risk of developing the condition, a new study finds.

item.title: Moscow targeted by drones while Russia bombards Kyiv
item.description: Ukraine launches its latest attack on the Russian capital, firing more than 400 drones, while the Kremlin deploys more ballistic missiles against targets across Ukraine.

item.title: Australias kids' TV quiz, part 1: Do you remember these shows?
item.description: What was the name of the tough, mangy cat in The Ferals? And which show featured EC, the faceless doll?

item.title: 'Narcissist' bosses are more likely to oppose work from home, studies show. But are they right?
item.description: Research published last month found narcissistic leaders — those with a tendency to be self-centred and entitled — were more likely than others to resist remote work. Why? The study concluded "it interferes with their motivations for power and status".

item.title: Royal wedding cellist Sheku Kanneh-Mason says music has power to teach young people empathy
item.description: Sheku Kanneh-Mason went viral performing at Prince Harry and Meghan Markle's wedding. He's captivated audiences since and is touring Australia this month.

item.title: How some Chinese EVs can have a new battery installed in about four minutes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Once an 'embarrassment' China's EV industry now leads the world
item.description: You can swap an empty electric vehicle battery in about four minutes in China. It is part of how China has come to dominate the EV industry.

item.title: Inquiry into violence against sex workers to examine stubborn stigma and legal solutions
item.description: A new inquiry into violence against sex workers in Australia and Thailand comes just weeks after the murder of Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla, allegedly by an Australian man.

item.title: Prepare for stock market pain as China throws a spanner into AI boom
item.description: Elon Musk's latest venture sent global stock markets to dizzying heights, but its performance since does not augur well for the company or the future of the AI boom.

item.title: Property market transforming with cold feet, cooling prices and withdrawals
item.description: Global instability, shifting tax policies and interest rate pressures force buyers and sellers to adjust to the changing property landscape.

item.title: Why Anthony Albanese would 'have a disagreement' with his younger self now
item.description: From left-wing roots to "radical centre" prime minister, Anthony Albanese reflects on his 30-year career in Parliament House.

item.title: Underwater gardening helping to restore Tasmania's lost kelp forests
item.description: A new live-in internship program on the Tasman Peninsula is working to repopulate the area's endangered giant kelp forests through underwater gardening.

item.title: Barnaby Joyce defends Pauline Hanson's Tommy Robinson meeting as dog interrupts interview
item.description: The One Nation MP says Pauline Hanson's meeting with far-right British activist Tommy Robinson did not amount to an endorsement of his criminal conduct, during a 7.30 interview that was twice interrupted by his dog.

item.title: Five years after Brisbane secured the 2032 Olympics, this is the state of play
item.description: Broken promises and scarcely broken ground. Five years since Brisbane secured the 2032 Olympic Games, this is the state of play.

item.title: Growers develop new purple passionfruit varieties in breeding breakthrough
item.description: An unpalatable future has been averted for Australians who love purple-skinned passionfruit, with new research ensuring better prospects for the delicious fruit.

item.title: Glittery poo unlocks fertility clues in rhinos at Monarto Safari Park
item.description: Edible glitter is being used to track the reproductive cycles of the two youngest southern white rhinos at Monarto Safari Park.

item.title: Security camera installation in homes is on the rise. Will it deter crime?
item.description: More home security cameras than ever before are being installed, but experts say it is not clear whether they are deterring criminals.

item.title: Zipporah 'Zippy' Fish on her breakout first season and having the best name in the AFLW
item.description: Zipporah "Zippy" Fish announced herself immediately on the AFLW stage with a breakout debut season.

item.title: Major milestone recorded as part of nationwide Bug Hunt, a win for citizen science
item.description: More than 1 million observations of insects, spiders, snails and other invertebrates have been recorded by citizen scientists as part of Bug Hunt Australia since October 2025.

item.title: Former NRL halfback Scott Dureau dies at 39 after long battle with cancer
item.description: Scott Dureau is remembered as an "exceptional" player and person after losing his life to brain cancer.

item.title: ACT public housing residents waiting weeks for heater repairs, say Canberra Liberals
item.description: Barbara Belmonte, an ACT public housing resident, has been waiting weeks for her heater to be repaired amid the sub-zero temperatures of a Canberra winter — and she is not the only one.

item.title: Protest held ahead of controversial child protection law changes to be debated in NT parliament
item.description: The Northern Territory parliament is debating controversial child protection laws that have been criticised for their potential impact on the Aboriginal Child Placement Principle.

item.title: Farmer Peter Chauncy's firewood side hustle fuels petrol station success
item.description: When bushfire and drought robbed farmer Peter Chauncy of his livelihood, he started chopping wood to sell. Forty years later, he runs one of Australia's largest firewood businesses.

item.title: 'That's Barnaby': Hanson laughs off Joyce interview interruption — as it happened
item.description: Pauline Hanson has said she was not advocating for the White Australia policy when referencing it in an interview with far-right podcaster Tommy Robinson last week.

item.title: Hundreds attend police meeting about Donald teen Layla Jeffery's alleged murder
item.description: Victoria Police and the Buloke Shire Council have held an event updating 300 Donald community members on the alleged murder of Layla Jeffery.

item.title: Prime minister backs bid for future Northern Territory AFL team, but no promises on funding
item.description: Anthony Albanese says he would love to see the creation of a Northern Territory AFL team while visiting the Tiwi Islands in the NT.

item.title: Kevin Keegan dies aged 75, football world 'devastated' by loss of English icon
item.description: Former Liverpool and Newcastle United star Kevin Keegan is remembered as one of English football's first rock stars after his death at 75.

item.title: Whistleblower's fight to expose decades of child abuse by Katanning Hostel rapist Dennis McKenna
item.description: Maggie Dawkins's efforts to shine a light on the horrific abuses committed by Dennis McKenna against a generation of boys in Katanning are set down in a new book.

item.title: Nine to cut jobs from metro newspaper newsrooms, ASX finishes flat — as it happened
item.description: Nine Entertainment shares were steady following news of redundancies at publications including The Age, The Sydney Morning Herald, Brisbane Times and WAtoday. On the ASX, the share market has finished flat after a negative session on Wall Street.

item.title: Reg Tayler named Australia's new women's rugby sevens coach ahead of 2028 LA Olympics
item.description: Rugby Australia makes a key appointment, as the world champion women's rugby sevens team looks ahead to the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

item.title: Teenager charged with lighting Cowra High School on fire and posting it on social media
item.description: A 17-year-old boy has been charged after allegedly breaking into his old school grounds and causing "extensive" damage to the site.

item.title: Papua New Guinea moves to outlaw AI deepfakes as victims call for more protection
item.description: Papua New Guinea prepares new laws to criminalise the malicious use of artificial intelligence, including deepfakes, voice cloning and digital impersonation.

item.title: Tiny red-fin blue-eye fish found only in Australia survives predator onslaught
item.description: With scoops made from milk bottles, volunteers have been working tirelessly to save the red-fin blue-eye, found nowhere else in the world, from extinction.

item.title: United Firefighters Union secretary accused of 'harassment' by FRV head
item.description: Victoria's fire rescue commissioner has written a strongly worded letter to the secretary of the United Firefighters Union, accusing him of aggressive and inappropriate communication amid a long-running conflict.

item.title: Spain's World Cup heroes greeted by millions at homecoming as FIFA probes ugly post-game fight
item.description: Spain's triumphant World Cup team's homecoming lights up Madrid as FIFA investigates a series of altercations that took place after the final whistle.

item.title: US to impose 50 per cent tariffs on most Canadian goods
item.description: The White House says the tariffs will go into effect in 30 days, meaning there is time for negotiations.

item.title: Nathan Buckley unlikely to join Essendon and Carlton coaching races after Tasmania snub
item.description: Nathan Buckley still harbours ambitions of coaching a second AFL team despite being overlooked for the Tasmania job.

item.title: Sixteen-year-old in custody suspected of killing mother, injuring little sister
item.description: Police say a teenage boy is suspected to have stabbed his mother to death and seriously injured his 12-year-old sister in an alleged family violence homicide in Perth's south-east.

item.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby child safety interim review makes seven recommendations
item.description: Nearly three months since the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, an interim report into her family circumstances prior to the tragedy has been handed down.

item.title: Australia's jobseeker system fails workers and enriches millionaires, report says
item.description: A new report argues Australia's employment services system is failing jobseekers, while filling the pockets of private equity firms and millionaires.

item.title: Jake Weatherald retained as Australia reveals Test squad for Bangladesh series
item.description: Jake Weatherald is retained in Australia's 13-player Test squad for the upcoming series against Bangladesh.

item.title: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson denies advocacy for 'version' of White Australia policy
item.description: Pauline Hanson angrily denies suggestions her strident opposition to migration by anyone from "radical Islamic countries" amounts to a version of the White Australia policy.

item.title: Diplomatic efforts seek to avert Trump split with UN refugee agency, sources say
item.description: The Trump administration is said to be weighing cutting ties with the UN refugee agency, prompting a campaign for the US to maintain support.

item.title: India steps up response to ongoing killing of Indian sailors by US and Iran
item.description: The Indian government is taking action after 10 sailors were killed and dozens more injured in attacks on commercial cargo ships and oil tankers during the battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Why more Australian men are choosing to be childfree
item.description: About 15 per cent of Australian men say they don't want children, an increase from 11 per cent in 2005. Ian says his own childhood played a large part in his decision.

item.title: Nicaragua's president extends rule, says country will not hold elections anytime soon
item.description: Daniel Ortega dashes opposition hopes that he might heed their demands for a democratic vote, effectively cancelled Nicaragua's next elections.

item.title: Zak Hepburn reviews The Odyssey and explains the art of IMAX filmmaking
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police investigate stabbing of 15-year-old at Cranbourne North
item.description: A boy was found with serious injuries and taken to hospital after an altercation between a group of teenagers.

item.title: Wyndham mayor Preet Singh resigns over sex offender character reference
item.description: Wyndham mayor Preet Singh says he showed "poor judgement" but also claims to have faced "abuse and intimidation" over the issue.

item.title: Tour de France team boss Jonathan Vaughters says night testing could have stopped cycling's blood-doping era
item.description: A former cyclist who took performance-enhancing drugs, now a team boss at the Tour de France, supports doping tests in the middle of the night at cycling's biggest race.

item.title: Surgical registration of Dr Liu-Ming Schmidt suspended after patient complaints
item.description: A six-month ban on Dr Liu-Ming Schmidt, who works across multiple hospitals in southern NSW, comes after 13 patient complaints including a missed cancer diagnosis and a "catastrophic error" that contributed to a patient's death.

item.title: Central Queensland University underpaid staff by $1.3 million, Fair Work finds
item.description: Central Queensland University signs an enforceable undertaking to repay more than a thousand staff members who were affected by underpayments that date back to the 2012 enterprise agreement.

item.title: Tradies and real estate agents among focus as police investigate alleged criminal syndicate
item.description: Victoria Police say they will be targeting anyone who organised or assisted with an alleged drug and money laundering operation for a state-wide criminal syndicate.

item.title: Isabella Nicholls remembered as police investigate fatal Sunshine Coast unit fire
item.description: A grieving Queensland family says it is grappling with the loss of a "beautiful" young mother as police continue to investigate the blaze.

item.title: US Army veteran arrested after FBI building 'attacked' during ICE protest
item.description: A former US Army soldier protesting Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has been arrested in New York after allegedly setting off fireworks, lighting a fire and shooting a pellet gun outside a federal office building.

item.title: WA Police probe use of force after handcuffed teenager injured during arrest
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Scam centres growing 'exponentially' despite Myanmar crackdown
item.description: The scam industry in Myanmar is morphing and heading into the jungle rather than disappearing since a crackdown began.

item.title: Author Randa Abdel-Fattah appears to abandon threatened defamation case against SA premier
item.description: Earlier this year, Palestinian-Australian author Randa Abdel-Fattah said she had sent two concerns notices to SA Premier Peter Malinauskas over comments made around the time of her controversial removal from the line-up of this year's Adelaide Writers' Week.

item.title: Tasmania's association of retired police question minister over delay of trauma support trial
item.description: Tasmania's association of retired police officers has questioned the police minister over his commitment to the wellbeing of members after the stalling of a much-awaited trial that would offer trauma support.

item.title: Brisbane mother says extremely rare identical quadruplets 'one giant miracle'
item.description: A maternal fetal medicine expert believes identical quadruplets born in Brisbane may be even rarer than one in 15 million, given they shared a placenta.

item.title: China, Philippines blame each other over South China Sea clash
item.description: Vision posted online by the Philippines Coast Guard shows Filipino and Chinese personnel briefly engaging each other from inflatable boats, with the Philippines accusing China of assaulting one of its navy staff.

item.title: Kids do feel the cold. So why won't they wear a jumper?
item.description: Do kids really not feel the cold like adults do? Or are they just expressing their independence? And when should you insist?

item.title: Pauline Hanson has denied advocacy for 'version' of White Australia policy
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Stingers star Abby Andrews building on Champions League, Olympics success at Sydney Water Polo World Cup
item.description: Winning the Champions League with Greek giants Olympiacos was "one of the coolest moments" of Abby Andrews's life. She wants to use that experience to push the Stingers to the next level.

item.title: Why supporters of India's 'Cockroach' movement marched on parliament
item.description: Thousands of protesters under the banner of the Cockroach Janta Party have marched on India's parliament and clashed with the authorities. Here's why.

item.title: Former Bishop of Broome Christopher Saunders faces first day of sex abuse trial
item.description: One of Australia's longest serving senior Catholic clerics had a sexual interest in young Aboriginal men and abused them in Western Australia's far north over several years, a Perth court hears.

item.title: Federal Labor MP Luke Gosling 'categorically denies' incident with NT administrator
item.description: Former army commando turned Labor MP, Luke Gosling, has denied involvement in an alleged incident with King Charles's representative in the NT.

item.title: AFL legend Tony Modra shares post-surgery photos after truck crash
item.description: AFL legend Tony Modra shares a post-crash update on social media, posting photos of him in hospital and with family, and saying he is now "getting out and about".

item.title: Hellenic Club found to have breached withdrawal regulations, cleared of failing to record problem gambling
item.description: The Hellenic Club has been cleared of breaches of failing to record the problem gambling of a Canberra couple despite the pair losing at least $345,000 on poker machines.

item.title: Two dead after house fire at Kingston, south of Brisbane
item.description: An investigation is underway into the cause of a house fire south of Brisbane.

item.title: Collingwood coach Craig McRae says Bobby Hill will 'always be welcome' at club as he gets set to cut contract
item.description: With Collingwood set to be at full strength for its Adelaide clash, coach Craig McRae sends a message to missing star Bobby Hill, who is set to quit the club.

item.title: Daily meals for Hobart's Risdon Prison shipped from Victoria as flooring fault shuts $19m kitchen
item.description: A newly built commercial kitchen at Hobart's Risdon Prison has shut just three months after officially opening due to construction faults, leaving staff to source daily meals for about 840 inmates from Victoria.

item.title: Indonesia sentences leader of baby-trafficking ring to seven years' jail
item.description: Lie Siu Luan, 70, was the leader of a group of 19 people accused of trafficking babies in Indonesia and Singapore.

item.title: Alessandra Galloni examines the implications of AI for journalism in 2026 Andrew Olle lecture
item.description: What will a trusted, sustainable, global news organisation look like in an era of agentic AI? And can we reinvent ourselves before someone else builds a less trustworthy, more dangerous alternative?

item.title: Hanson's European travel benefactor still a mystery, but One Nation leader says it was not taxpayers
item.description: In her first appearance since returning to Australia, Pauline Hanson fields questions about her trip to Europe with billionaire Gina Rinehart.

item.title: Inquest to determine whether Adelaide man killed his mother and staged her death
item.description: An inquest is investigating whether a man killed his mother before placing her in the driver's seat of her car to stage that she was behind the wheel during a crash.

item.title: China's EV Revolution
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: AI companies 'stole' Australian culture, news bosses say as they call for licensing deals at Andrew Olle lecture
item.description: The heads of the ABC, SBS, Nine and News Corp say AI companies should strike licensing deals rather than freely train on Australian news, warning journalism is a public good that must be protected.

item.title: Italians protest after man dies during police arrest in Bologna
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giogia Meloni is calling for a full investigation into the death of a man in the city of Bologna that sparked anti-police protests.

item.title: Russia conducts live-fire weapons exercise off British coast, UK defence ministry says
item.description: A UK minister has confirmed that a Russian naval vessel conducted a "live-fire weapons exercise" in international waters off the coast of Plymouth in south-west England.

item.title: Killed UK politician Ann Widdecombe hit 21 times with hammer, prosecutors say
item.description: Joshua Kerry, 28, appears in the Westminster Magistrates' Court charged with murder over a killing that has shocked the public and political establishment.

item.title: Trump says US will target suspected Iranian nuclear development site 'pretty soon'
item.description: Donald Trump says the United States is far from finished with Iran, warning its next military target could be an underground nuclear complex.

item.title: Trump says US will target suspected Iranian nuclear development site 'pretty soon'
item.description: Donald Trump says the United States is far from finished with Iran, warning its next military target could be an underground nuclear complex.

item.title: Travel quiz, July 22: Join us for a trip around the world
item.description: Where are macadamia nuts indigenous to? And what’s the capital city of Switzerland?

item.title: Penny Lane is Dead pairs gory horror with a queer love story
item.description: After spending years in special effects and make-up, filmmaker Mia'Kate Russell's ocker nightmare Penny Lane Is Dead has made it to the silver screen.

item.title: Family of seven's home uninhabitable after Suncorp insurance delay exacerbates damage
item.description: A Queensland family is living in an overcrowded caravan and shed after insurance provider Suncorp exacerbated damage to their home, initially caused by a leak.

item.title: Jewish schools say kids hurting as security getting in way of education
item.description: The Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion hears from Jewish school leaders about how they need to implement extraordinary and expensive security measures.

item.title: Labor members revive push for national gambling regulator
item.description: Labor members and unions are gearing up for fights over gambling, gas and AI at the party's national conference later this week.

item.title: How the Australian Stingers women's water polo team gains strength through bonds of friendship
item.description: Australia's women's water polo team, the Stingers, were the most successful Australian team at the Paris Olympics, winning a stunning silver medal. This is the secret to their success.

item.title: Albanese survived three near-fatal car accidents. His career is just as unlikely
item.description: Anthony Albanese has been in near-fatal vehicle accidents not once, but three times. Politically, his is also a story of survival against the odds.

item.title: Here's what you need to know about the Victorian teachers strike tomorrow
item.description: Victorian state school teachers and educators are going ahead with their second 24-hour strike this year after union members rejected the government's latest pay offer.

item.title: Artificial intelligence company Firmus plans to use state-owned agricultural irrigation water
item.description: Data centre builder Firmus posted a statement, since removed, indicating its intention to get some of its cooling water from the state-owned irrigation system, adding "but we do not want to highlight this yet".

item.title: Woman's family wrongly told she was dead in hospital identity error
item.description: A Melbourne hospital apologises after mixing up the medical data of two people, resulting in one of them being wrongly declared dead by police.

item.title: JackJumpers Roth's advice for inaugural Tasmania Devils AFL coach Ken Hinkley
item.description: Being out amongst the people will be key for incoming Tasmania Devils coach Ken Hinkley, according to the man who led the state to NBL glory in 2024.

item.title: Upgrades to Queensland's Triple Zero phone system delayed until next year due to Iran war
item.description: The Iran war has been a contributing factor to Queensland's Triple Zero phone system being delayed until next year, the ABC understands.

item.title: BlueScope Steel hit with multiple workplace safety breaches after contractor's death
item.description: There have been six incidents involving falling tools and building materials at BlueScope, less than a year since contractor Jack McGrath was killed.

item.title: Whyalla workers injured amid attempts to restart steelworks' blast furnace
item.description: Three Whyalla steelworks employees have been treated for injuries suffered during attempts to restart the site's troubled blast furnace, the ABC understands.

item.title: Outback reunions cancelled by rain damage as tourism suffers for a second year
item.description: Flood-damaged roads and soggy racetracks mean some of the outback's favourite events are being cancelled and keeping tourists away.

item.title: Family of homeless man who died outside Sydney train station prepare for final farewell
item.description: The mother of a homeless man whose death outside a Sydney train station went unnoticed for days is pleading for her son's remains to be returned to Nepal.

item.title: Rebel Wilson awaits ruling in defamation battle with fellow actor
item.description: Hollywood star Rebel Wilson will learn today whether she defamed the lead actor on her "fun girl-power" film, in a judgment to be handed down in the Federal Court.

item.title: Hegseth reveals $US37.5b cost of Iran war during grilling over testosterone policy, troop deaths
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is being questioned on the cost of the war against Iran, his idea of testosterone screening for troops, and the number of service people who have died.

item.title: Players and fans made this World Cup a success, but the politics shouldn't be forgotten
item.description: The biggest ever World Cup brought diverse communities together and galvanised its non-traditional footballing co-hosts, but the concerns raised from the start have not disappeared in the positive afterglow.

item.title: Live: Minister issues secret phone warning ahead of national alert test
item.description: Emergency Management Minister Kristy McBain urges people with secret or concealed mobile phones to turn them off or put them on aeroplane mode ahead of Monday's national alert test. Follow live.

item.title: Elusive fogbow photographed at Cobar, western NSW
item.description: Cold weather and moist conditions combine to produce a fogbow in an unlikely place.

item.title: Live: ASX rises as oil prices hit five-week high, Wall Street tech stocks rebound
item.description: The Australian share market has started its day higher after Wall Street's beaten-down tech and semiconductor stocks rebounded sharply overnight. Oil prices surged to a five-week high as Donald Trump signalled the US is "not finished" attacking Iran. Follow live.

item.title: Bradley died months after national antipsychotic medicine shortages, coronial inquest hears
item.description: Bradley Buswell, aged in his 40s and living with schizophrenia, died in state care after his medication became unavailable due to national shortages. An ongoing coronial inquest is probing the circumstances of his death.

item.title: AusAlert emergency warning system test to go ahead despite rollout concerns
item.description: State and territory representatives had voiced significant concerns about the rollout of the national emergency warning system in a meeting with the minister Kristy McBain.

item.title: Brandon McNulty victim of road rage incident at Tour de France as Remco Evenepoel wins time trial
item.description: Brandon McNulty is confronted by an angry driver on his way to the time trial as Remco Evenepoel shaves some time off Tadej Pogačar's overall lead.

item.title: Sunshine Coast police declare crime scene after firefighters find body in shed
item.description: Police have declared a crime scene after a man's body was found in the charred remains of a shed in Nambour, in the second fatal fire on the Sunshine Coast in four days.

item.title: Cameron McEvoy returns to Glasgow for Commonwealth Games after almost giving up on swimming
item.description: As one of just two Australian swimmers returning to Glasgow for a second Commonwealth Games, Cameron McEvoy is taking time to appreciate how much has happened to him in the past 12 years.

item.title: NT government passes child protection reforms in 'significant overhaul'
item.description: The Northern Territory parliament has passed legislative changes to the child protection system, with the Country Liberal Party (CLP) describing its new laws as "the most significant overhaul of the Territory's child protection system in a generation".

item.title: Boy, 16, charged with murdering mother, attempting to kill sister at Bentley home
item.description: A 16-year-old boy is charged with murdering his mother and attempting to kill his sister in an alleged domestic violence homicide in suburban Perth.

item.title: Zelenskyy replaces Ukrainian military chief after sacking popular defence minister
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy replaces the country's commander-in-chief after protests against his sacking of the defence minister last week.

item.title: $30 million biochar facility proposed in central Queensland with Rio Tinto a major buyer
item.description: Rio Tinto has signed an agreement with an Australian company for a biochar product that could help replace coal used in its emissions-intensive Queensland refineries.

item.title: FIFA president Gianni Infantino accused of 'destroying the football industry' with World Cup expansion
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino comes in for more heavy criticism, this time at the hands of La Liga boss Javier Tebas.

item.title: Mother and a family friend killed in house fire in Kingston south of Brisbane
item.description: Leanne Gay Laver, 62, and family friend Phillip Gordon, 73, died when the house caught alight in the Logan suburb of Kingston.

item.title: Tourism businesses worried about drop in winter visitors in WA's south
item.description: Despite being an idyllic location for a winter getaway, businesses in WA's Great Southern have seen a decline in tourists this season.

item.title: Train station car parking in Melbourne's west becomes election issue
item.description: With only months to the Victorian election, Melton Council is calling on both sides of politics to commit to two new train stations in the fast-growing municipality.

item.title: Berkeley Vale mother Louise Farley took up soccer for her dying son
item.description: After Spencer Farley was diagnosed with terminal cancer, he asked his mum to start playing soccer. In the 12 months since his death, the game has kept her going.
